aronui.when_to_use: Use this tool to create scheduled automation tasks for one-time reminders or recurring schedules. It is suitable when you have all required information (name, prompt, frequency, time) and have checked for existing automations first using list_automations.
aronui.output_shape: The tool returns a string: either a success confirmation (e.g., 'Automation created successfully with ID <id>') or an error message describing the failure.

pitfalls.text: Missing required field 'prompt': error text seen: 'Error: No prompt provided for automation.'
Missing or invalid 'frequency': error text seen: 'Error: frequency '' is not valid. Must be one of: once, hourly, daily, weekly, monthly. Ask the user which frequency they want — NEVER assume daily.'
Empty or invalid 'time' format: error text seen: 'Error: Invalid time format ''. Expected HH:MM format (e.g., '22:46').'
Omitting 'time' entirely may cause silent default (e.g., 06:00) leading to duplicate time conflict: error text seen: 'ERROR: Automation already exists at the same time! ...'
Duplicate automation with same name and time: error text seen: 'ERROR: Automation already exists with same name and time! ...'

procedure: 1. Call list_automations first to check if a similar automation already exists.
2. If a similar automation exists, use read_automation + update_automation instead of creating a new one.
3. Ask the user for any missing required information (prompt, frequency, time, and task-specific details like city) in the user's language.
4. Ensure frequency is explicitly chosen: use 'once' for any one-time task (reminders, delayed actions). Only use 'daily'/'weekly'/'monthly'/'hourly' when the user explicitly requests recurrence.
5. Provide time in exactly HH:MM format (e.g., 09:00). Avoid scheduling within 10 minutes of an existing automation.
6. In the prompt, include an instruction to send the result to the user via their main_messenger (using the appropriate send_* tool).
7. Call create_automation with all required fields: name, prompt, frequency, time.
8. After creation, optionally verify by listing automations or reading the new automation.
verification: Verify that the automation was created by calling list_automations to see the new entry with the correct name, frequency, and time.
Alternatively, use read_automation with the automation ID to confirm the details.
If an error occurs (e.g., duplicate conflict), handle it by choosing a different time or updating the existing automation instead.
